what_it_does: Defines a browser-global React-style icon renderer that reads SVG bodies from window.CONCEPTV_ICONS, renders named icons with configurable size, color, stroke weight, background substitution, and fallback placeholder output, plus a circular badge wrapper component.
observations: ConceptVIcon looks up icon definitions by name from window.CONCEPTV_ICONS.
Missing icon names render a dashed placeholder square with text showing the missing name.
The component injects SVG markup via dangerouslySetInnerHTML from def.body.
The bg prop can replace var(--cv-icon-bg, #fff) text inside the SVG body and also sets a CSS custom property on the SVG style.
ConceptVBadge renders an inline-flex circular span using gold-themed default CSS variables and nests ConceptVIcon inside it.
Both components are assigned to window for global browser access.
standouts: Composable icon rendering is centralized around a named runtime icon registry.
The missing-icon placeholder makes unresolved names visible at render time.
ConceptVBadge packages the same icon renderer into a brand-styled circular entity badge.
conventions: Uses default prop values in function parameter destructuring.
Uses currentColor and CSS custom properties for theme-aware icon coloring.
Uses inline styles for wrapper badge layout and presentation.
Publishes components onto window instead of using module exports.
Uses JSX with browser script loading described in comments.
concerns: Rendering depends on browser globals and script load order.
dangerouslySetInnerHTML relies on the icon registry body strings being valid and appropriate for direct SVG injection.
The background substitution is a string replacement tied to one exact CSS variable pattern.
The fallback text includes the icon name inside a very small SVG text element, which may not remain legible for longer names.
notes: This file is the runtime bridge between a global SVG path registry and JSX-facing ConceptV icon and badge components.
questions: What complete schema is expected for each window.CONCEPTV_ICONS entry beyond a body property?
Is the visible missing-icon placeholder intended for all environments?
Should badge accessibility attributes or labels be supplied by callers through rest props or handled here?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose as a composable icon renderer matches the implementation, with an added convenience badge component.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to make a shared icon library usable from JSX without importing individual SVGs, while allowing theme color, stroke weight, and badge presentation variations at call sites.
novelty: The combination of a global icon registry, direct SVG body injection, placeholder rendering for unknown names, and a branded circular badge wrapper is the distinct local pattern.
contribution: Provides a reusable named-icon rendering surface, missing-icon visibility behavior, CSS-variable-aware background handling, and a circular brand badge wrapper that a fused system would keep as icon delivery and presentation capabilities.
summary_for_embedding: A JSX file defining ConceptVIcon and ConceptVBadge, where ConceptVIcon renders named SVG bodies from window.CONCEPTV_ICONS with configurable styling and fallback output, and ConceptVBadge wraps icons in a circular branded badge.
intention_for_embedding: This was for exposing ConceptV design-system iconography as composable browser-global JSX components backed by a preloaded icon path registry.

symbols.does: Renders a named SVG icon from window.CONCEPTV_ICONS with configurable size, color, stroke weight, background handling, style, and passthrough props.
symbols.description: ConceptVIcon is the core renderer for this file. It resolves an icon definition by name, injects the SVG body into a 24x24 SVG shell, applies stroke and sizing defaults, performs optional background token replacement, and shows a dashed placeholder when the name is unknown.

symbols.does: Renders a circular inline badge with themed background, border, and a nested ConceptVIcon.
symbols.description: ConceptVBadge is a presentation wrapper for placing ConceptVIcon inside a gold-themed circular badge. It computes a default inner icon size from the badge size, applies inline flex centering and circular styling, and passes the selected name and color through to ConceptVIcon.
